\boxed{G}

The correct answer is **G. Akitu**, which refers to the ancient Babylonian New Year festival celebrated over ten days, marking the vernal equinox and honoring the god Marduk. This festival was central to Babylonian culture and included religious rituals, processions, and the renewal of royal authority. The other options correspond to different cultures or festivals (e.g., Nowruz is Persian, Songkran is Thai).
